children.label: CHC33021: Certificate III in Individual Support
CHC33021 Certificate III in Individual Support (Ageing)
CHC43015: Certificate IV in Ageing Support
CHC43121: Certificate IV in Disability Support
CHC33021 Certificate III in Individual Support (Disability)

courses.durationLeft: Up to 12‑18 months (depending on pace, location)
courses.heading: CHC33021: Certificate III in Individual Support (Ageing & Disability)
courses.description: This qualification reflects the role of individuals in community, home or residential care settings who provide person‑centred support under supervision, following an individualised plan.

points.text: Provide person‑centred support to people requiring care due to ageing or disability
Assist with daily living activities in home, community or residential settings
Work under supervision and delegation within a multidisciplinary team

courses.heading: CHC43015: Certificate IV in Ageing Support
courses.description: This qualification reflects the role of support workers in aged services (residential, home or community) who complete specialised tasks and functions, coordinate services and may have limited supervisory responsibilities.

points.text: Coordinate person‑centred services for older people
Support people living with dementia and complex needs
Provide leadership and/or supervisory support in aged care services

courses.heading: CHC43121: Certificate IV in Disability Support
courses.description: This qualification reflects the role of individuals in community settings or people’s homes who provide support in a manner that empowers people with disability to achieve greater levels of independence and community participation.

points.text: Provide advanced person‑centred support for people with disability
Work with individualised plans and community participation goals
Support independence and inclusion for people with disability

courses.heading: HLTAID009: Provide Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR)
courses.description: This course provides essential skills and knowledge for performing cardiopulmonary resuscitation and responding to breathing and cardiac emergencies in workplace or community settings in line with ARC guidelines.

points.text: Recognise and respond to cardiac arrest and choking
Meet workplace compliance for CPR and first‑aid readiness

courses.heading: HLTAID011: Provide First Aid
courses.description: This course delivers comprehensive training in first aid response, life support and casualty management until professional medical help arrives. It covers CPR, injury management and responding to common emergencies in workplace and community settings.

points.text: Provide basic life support and casualty management
Manage injury, illness and emergency situations

courses.heading: CHC33021 Certificate III in Individual Support (Ageing)
courses.description: This qualification prepares learners to deliver safe, compassionate and person-centred care to older individuals across residential, community and home-based environments. It focuses on empowering independence, supporting daily living needs and promoting dignity and wellbeing within aged-care settings.

points.text: Work effectively using a person-centred, dignity-driven approach
Support older individuals with daily living, mobility and wellbeing
Follow safe work practices, infection control and communication standards

courses.heading: CHC33021 Certificate III in Individual Support (Disability)
courses.description: This qualification equips learners with the skills to provide inclusive, empowering and person-directed support to individuals with disability. It emphasises communication, independent living assistance, rights-based practice and safe, professional conduct across community and home settings.

points.text: Deliver person-centred support aligned with individual goals and abilities
Promote independence, self-advocacy and participation in daily life
Apply safe work practices, communication strategies and duty-of-care principles

resources.heading: Financial Planning Guide

facts.heading: Job-Ready Training

facts.heading: Industry Partnerships

facts.heading: Student Support
facts.text: Receive guidance from our dedicated team throughout.

testimonialData.name: Michael Thompson
testimonialData.review: Bayside Nursing College gave me the practical skills and confidence to start my career in aged care immediately after graduation.

testimonialData.name: Sophie Lee
testimonialData.review: The workplace placements were invaluable. I gained real-world experience that helped me secure my first support role.

testimonialData.name: Daniel White
testimonialData.review: Trainers were professional, approachable, and shared genuine insights from the field. Highly recommended for anyone entering care work.

testimonialData.name: Emily Carter
testimonialData.review: I felt fully supported throughout my course, from study advice to practical placements. I’m now confidently working in disability support.

missionData.heading: Our Mission
missionData.text: To provide high-quality vocational education that leads to safe practice, ethical conduct, and strong employment prospects in aged care, disability support, and community services.
points: Collaboration with employers to meet needs

missionData.heading: Our Vision
missionData.text: To be a trusted training provider where care workers feel valued, deliver high standards of support, and continue learning as their roles grow.
points: Keep trainers current with best practices

missionData.heading: Our Values
missionData.text: We uphold respect, empathy, inclusion, and ethical practice in every aspect of education and care delivery.
points: Treat all individuals with respect and dignity
Ensure integrity and fairness in assessment
Collaborate with industry to stay relevant

faqs.question: Is Bayside Nursing College a registered training organisation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Bayside Nursing College is fully ASQA-registered (RTO Code: 45929), which means all our courses meet national standards for vocational education. Being a registered training organisation ensures that your qualifications are recognised across Australia and comply with industry requirements. This gives you confidence that your study investment is credible and valuable for future employment.

faqs.question: Are your courses recognised by employers?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Our courses are nationally recognised and highly regarded by employers in healthcare and community services. Graduates are equipped with practical skills, industry knowledge, and professional competencies that make them job-ready. Employers trust our programs because they align with national standards and incorporate real-world training.

faqs.question: Do your courses include work placement?
faqs.answer: Yes. All of our programs include mandatory work placements to provide hands-on experience in real healthcare settings. These placements help students develop practical skills, build professional confidence, and network with potential employers. They bridge the gap between theory and practice, ensuring you are ready to contribute effectively from your first day on the job.

faqs.question: Can I study part-time or online?
faqs.answer: Yes. We understand that many students balance work, family, and study commitments. That’s why we offer flexible delivery options, including part-time, online, and blended learning modes. You can complete coursework at your own pace while still benefiting from in-person practical sessions to gain hands-on experience.

faqs.question: What kind of jobs can I get after completing Certificate III in Individual Support?
faqs.answer: Graduates of Certificate III in Individual Support can pursue careers as personal care assistants, home care workers, or aged care support staff. Our programs provide the practical skills and knowledge employers look for, including patient care, communication, and safety procedures, giving you a strong foundation to advance in the healthcare sector.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete a course?
faqs.answer: Course duration varies depending on the program and study mode. Full-time students typically complete a Certificate III in Individual Support in around 6–12 months, while part-time or online learners may take longer. Our flexible scheduling ensures you can progress at a pace that suits your personal and professional commitments, without compromising learning quality.

faqs.question: What support services are available for students?
faqs.answer: Bayside Nursing College offers comprehensive support services, including academic assistance, career guidance, counselling, and study skills workshops. Our dedicated staff help students succeed academically and professionally, while also providing support for personal challenges to ensure a positive learning experience throughout your course.

faqs.question: Are there any prerequisites to enrol in your courses?
faqs.answer: Most of our courses require a minimum of a high school certificate or equivalent. Some programs may have additional requirements, such as language proficiency or police checks for work placements. We provide clear guidance during the enrolment process to ensure you meet all requirements and are prepared for the course.

faqs.question: Do you offer career support after graduation?
faqs.answer: Yes. We provide ongoing career support for graduates, including job placement assistance, resume and interview coaching, and networking opportunities with employers in healthcare and community services. Our goal is to ensure that students transition successfully from study to employment, with the confidence and skills to thrive in their chosen career.

coursesFaqs.question: Can I study part-time or online?
coursesFaqs.answer: Yes. We understand that many students balance work, family, and study commitments. That’s why we offer flexible delivery options, including part-time, online, and blended learning modes. You can complete coursework at your own pace while still benefiting from in-person practical sessions to gain hands-on experience. This flexibility ensures that you do not compromise your career or personal responsibilities while achieving your educational goals.

coursesFaqs.question: What kind of jobs can I get after completing Certificate III in Individual Support?
coursesFaqs.answer: Graduates of Certificate III in Individual Support can pursue careers as personal care assistants, home care workers, or aged care support staff. Our programs provide the practical skills and knowledge employers look for, including patient care, communication, and safety procedures, giving you a strong foundation to advance in the healthcare sector. Graduates may also explore roles in disability services, community support, and home-based care, depending on their elective units and specialization.

coursesFaqs.question: How long does it take to complete a course?
coursesFaqs.answer: Course duration varies depending on the program and study mode. Full-time students typically complete a Certificate III in Individual Support in around 6–12 months, while part-time or online learners may take longer. Our flexible scheduling ensures you can progress at a pace that suits your personal and professional commitments, without compromising learning quality. The duration also includes mandatory work placement hours, allowing you to gain hands-on experience in real healthcare settings.

coursesFaqs.question: What support services are available for students?
coursesFaqs.answer: Bayside Nursing College offers comprehensive support services, including academic assistance, career guidance, counselling, and study skills workshops. Our dedicated staff help students succeed academically and professionally, while also providing support for personal challenges to ensure a positive learning experience throughout your course. Additional resources include online libraries, access to mentoring, and peer support networks to encourage collaboration and knowledge sharing.

coursesFaqs.question: Are your courses nationally recognised?
coursesFaqs.answer: Yes. All our courses are fully accredited and nationally recognised under the Australian Qualifications Framework. This means your qualifications meet rigorous quality standards and are recognised by employers across Australia. Graduating from our courses ensures that your skills are credible, up-to-date with industry standards, and transferrable to a variety of healthcare and community service settings.

coursesFaqs.question: Do your courses include work placement?
coursesFaqs.answer: Yes. All our programs include mandatory work placements to provide hands-on experience in real healthcare settings. These placements help students develop practical skills, build professional confidence, and network with potential employers. The work placement component is carefully supervised and structured to ensure that you gain meaningful exposure to the work environment, complementing the theoretical knowledge acquired in the classroom and online modules.

coursesFaqs.question: Are there any prerequisites to enrol in your courses?
coursesFaqs.answer: Most of our courses require a minimum of a high school certificate or equivalent. Some programs may have additional requirements, such as language proficiency, police checks, or immunisation records for work placements. We provide clear guidance during the enrolment process to ensure you meet all requirements and are prepared for the course. This ensures that all students have the necessary foundation to succeed in both theory and practical components.

coursesFaqs.question: Do you offer career support after graduation?
coursesFaqs.answer: Yes. We provide ongoing career support for graduates, including job placement assistance, resume and interview coaching, and networking opportunities with employers in healthcare and community services. Our goal is to ensure that students transition successfully from study to employment, equipped with confidence, professional skills, and access to a network of industry contacts. Career workshops and alumni events are also held to support lifelong learning and career advancement.

coursesFaqs.question: How are courses assessed?
coursesFaqs.answer: Assessment is competency-based and includes a combination of written assignments, online quizzes, practical demonstrations, and work placement evaluations. This approach ensures that students not only understand theoretical concepts but can also apply them in practical, real-world scenarios. Feedback is provided regularly to support continuous improvement, and students must meet all competency requirements to successfully complete the course and receive their nationally recognised qualification.

courses.title: CHC33021: Certificate III in Individual Support
courses.description: This qualification equips learners with the skills and knowledge to provide person-centred care and support to individuals in aged care, disability, or community settings. It focuses on promoting independence, dignity, and well-being in daily living activities.

courses.title: CHC43015: Certificate IV in Ageing Support
courses.description: This course develops advanced competencies for supporting older individuals in residential, community, or home care environments. Learners gain expertise in service coordination, individualized support, and person-centred care for ageing populations.

points.text: Lead and supervise aged care teams

courses.title: CHC43121: Certificate IV in Disability Support
courses.description: This qualification prepares students to empower people with disabilities by promoting independence, inclusion, and well-being. It covers behaviour support, communication strategies, and advanced person-centred care techniques.

courses.title: HLTAID009: Provide Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR)
courses.description: This course provides essential skills and knowledge to perform cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) in line with current Australian Resuscitation Council (ARC) guidelines. It focuses on responding to breathing and cardiac emergencies confidently and effectively.

points.text: Respond to cardiac and breathing emergencies

courses.title: HLTAID011: Provide First Aid
courses.description: This qualification delivers comprehensive training in first aid response, life support, and casualty management until professional medical help arrives. It includes CPR, injury management, and response to common emergencies in workplaces and communities.

points.text: Provide life support and first aid

courses.title: CHC43015: Certificate IV in Ageing Support
courses.description: This course develops advanced competencies for supporting older individuals in residential, community, or home care environments. Learners gain expertise in service coordination, individualized support, and person-centred care for ageing populations.

points.text: Enhance leadership skills in care settings

confidence.title: Affordable Study
confidence.paragraph: Access high-quality nursing education without financial stress. Our programs provide exceptional value for students from all backgrounds. Take advantage of cost-effective tuition structures, flexible payment plans, and scholarship opportunities to invest in your future without compromise.

confidence.title: Flexible Learning
confidence.paragraph: Study on your schedule with programs designed to adapt seamlessly to your lifestyle. Whether you are a working professional, parent, or balancing other commitments, our flexible delivery modes—including online, on-campus, and blended learning—ensure you can progress at your own pace without compromising quality.

confidence.title: Workplace Commitment
confidence.paragraph: Gain practical, real-world experience through structured programs that prepare you for a successful career. Our partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and industry professionals ensure you graduate with both theoretical knowledge and hands-on skills to confidently enter the workforce.

confidence.title: Nationally Recognized
confidence.paragraph: Our programs adhere to national standards, ensuring your qualifications are respected and valued across Australia. Graduates benefit from industry-aligned curricula, accredited certifications, and recognition that opens doors to professional opportunities nationwide.
